title: Airway physical examination tests for detection of difficult airway management in apparently normal adult patients
research_question: What is the diagnostic accuracy of Mallampati classification and other airway examination tests for detecting difficult airway in adult patients with no apparent anatomical airway abnormalities?

pico.population: adult patients with no apparent anatomical airway abnormalities
pico.intervention: Mallampati classification and other commonly used airway examination tests
pico.comparator: reference standard for difficult airway assessment
pico.outcome: diagnostic accuracy for difficult face mask ventilation, difficult laryngoscopy, difficult tracheal intubation, and failed intubation

inclusion_criteria.text: Study evaluates airway physical examination tests for difficult airway detection
Study reports diagnostic accuracy measures (sensitivity, specificity, AUC)
Adult patients (18 years or older) with apparently normal airway anatomy
Evaluates Mallampati classification or other airway examination tests (thyromental distance, sternomental distance, etc.)

exclusion_criteria.text: Pediatric patients (under 18 years) or patients with known anatomical airway abnormalities
